titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Terry Tempest Williams Photography Collection
titlestmt.author: Gladys García

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday February 14, 2026 at 23:14

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 30-31: In-process material, pending conservation treatment/housing. For further information, consult Access Services.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, October 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Collected photographs, 1899-2020. II. Portraits, circa 1940-2017, with small grouping of personal photogrpahs. Existing folder titles will be maintained where present.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Terry Tempest Williams (born 1955) is an American writer, poet, naturalist, and environmental and social activist. Her writings link environmental causes with social justice and women's health, art, politics, and spirituality. Williams has drawn much of her inspiration from the natural landscapes across the United States, especially the American Southwest. Williams has a long history of environmental activism, including her and her husband, Brooke Williams, buying Bureau of Land Management oil and gas leases in Utah in 2015 as a conservation effort. Willams and her husband, live between Castle Valley, Utah and Jackson Hole, Wyoming.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Terry Tempest Williams Photography Collection, Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Terry Tempest Williams papers (WA MSS S-2700).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection mostly consists of landscape photography collected by Terry Tempest Williams over the course of her career and life, as well as professional portraits of Williams herself. Most of the photographs are directly related to Williams' creative projects or projects she has consulted on. The collection contains a small amount of personal photographs. The collection contains various photographic formats such as digital, inkjet, gelatin, thermal, and a printed book. Thematic elements of collection span environmentalism, animal welfare, nuclear war, spirituality, and the American Southwest. Collection contains letter from Williams reflecting on photographs in collection, see Series I for more information.
The first series of the collection contains photographs of survivors of the Rwandan genocide, depicting heavy scarring on survivors' bodies.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Terry Tempest Williams photograph collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscripts Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains professional photographs collected by or gifted to Terry Tempest Williams over approximately five decades. Some photographs are directly related to projects Williams collaborated on or served as a consultant. Photographs in the series depict natural landscapes across the American Southwest, and include themes of environmental degradation, activism, the Vietnam War, nuclear technology and more. Formats present are inkjet, digital prints, and silver gelatin. First folder in series contains letter from Williams reflecting on photographs in collection.
Contains photographs of survivors of the Rwandan genocide in The Rwanda Healing Project (2007) by Chris Noble, depicting heavy scarring on survivors' bodies.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 30-31: In-process material, pending conservation treatment/housing. For further information, consult Beinecke Rare Books and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs related to Terry Tempest Williams essay Still.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image used in The Hour of Land 2016.

did.unittitle: Fajada Butte, Chaco Culture National Historical Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See related essay Kinship in Pilgrimage 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See related writing Winged Resistance (2017) by Terry Tempest Williams.

title.part: Melting Ice, Ice Pattern #19, Oxford, MA

scopecontent.p: Part of Waterforms (2016) .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Doña Mercedes Formica de Llosent y Marañon, Female Barrister on Balcony in Madrid, Spain.

scopecontent.p: Photo of Frida Kahlo with her pet deer Granizo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Part of People and Places of Tuscany photograph series.

did.unittitle: Fat man with Edith [Atomic bomb], from Critical Mass

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Part of the Millenial Forest/Trees at Sea project, 1999-2004, exploring ancient growth forests of the United States of America and Vietnam. Photo was taken near Bishop, CA, with tree purported to 4,700 years old. Printed digitally with vegetable inks on mulberry paper coated with ground mica and gum Arabic.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 30-31: In-process material, pending conservation treatment/housing. For further information, consult Access Services.

did.unittitle: Snowy Owl, Barrow, Alaska, Artic Ocean

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See related essay The Glorious Indifference to Wilderness (2014).

title.part: The Moon is Behind Us: 30 Letters in Response to 30 Moons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book co-written with Terry Tempest Williams reflecting on the Covid-19 pandemic, inspiration for The Moon Behind Us (2021).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs for co-written book of the same name by Sherry and Williams.

did.unittitle: Karl Malone, Utah Jazz for Desert News

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to co-written book Coyote's Canyon (1989).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 30-31: In-process material, pending conservation treatment/housing. For further information, consult Access Services.

did.unittitle: …But I Just Know There is a Mystical Experience Around Here Somewhere

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See related essay by Williams, Avian Primitives 2008.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gifts from conservationist Ian Cumming and Trish Brandt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains professional photographic portraits of Terry Tempest Williams, she and her spouse Brooke Williams, and portraits of Williams with her family. Most portraits were shot on location in or near (her former home) Salt Lake City, UT; Castle Valley, UT; and Jackson Hole, WY. The series also includes personal photographs of Williams as a child, with her mother (Diane Dixon Tempest), family, friends, and colleagues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image used for The Hour of Land 2016.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images for Refuge (1991), taken near Great Salt Lake, Utah.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos of artwork by Clifford Bryclea for Pieces of White Shell and photos of Williams at Utah Museum of Natural History for Salt Lake Tribune .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images used for book jacket, Pieces of White Shell (1987).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images used for The Illuminated Desert (2008), taken at Arches National Park, Moab, Utah.
